articles.title: Dollar and Precious Metals Slump on Hawkish Central Banks
articles.expand: The dollar index (DXY00 ) on Thursday fell by -0.75%. The dollar was under pressure Thursday after the British pound, the euro, and the Japanese yen all rallied following hawkish comments from the BOE, ECB, and BOJ on the inflationary effects of soaring energy prices from the war in Iran....

articles.title: EUR/USD surges over 1% as ECB rate hike talk hits USD
articles.expand: EUR/USD reverses course and rises by more than 1.16% after the European Central Bank (ECB) held rates unchanged, while a leaked source revealed that policymakers are ready to discuss rate hikes as soon as April.

articles.title: Gold falls on rising yields; tentative rebound seen toward $4,800

Summary: Gold fell as Middle East tensions pushed oil higher and lifted inflation fears Higher-for-longer rate expectations drove yields and the US dollar up Rising real yields pressured gold despite geopolitical risks Profit-taking amplified the move after a strong pri...

articles.title: Another chaotic post-FOMC session – North American Session Market Wrap for March 19
articles.expand: March 19th 2026 North-American Session Recap – Markets took a totally different direction after yesterday's FOMC and recent announcements. Check up the latest trends and developments, daily asset and FX performance, what changed fundamentally and what's coming up in the session ahead.

articles.title: NZD/USD rallies despite weak New Zealand GDP and Fed hawkish hold
articles.expand: The NZD/USD pair surges to the 0.5870 level, up 1.30% on Thursday amid broad weakness of the US Dollar (USD) despite the Federal Reserve (Fed) holding decision the previous day and weak economic data in New Zealand .

articles.title: Economic and event calendar in Asia 20 March 2026 - PBoC Loan Prime Rate (LPR) setting
articles.expand: The People's Bank of China rate setting is a non-event. China's main policy rate is now the reverse repo rate, currently at 1.4% for the 7-day. The 7-day rate serves as a key policy benchmark, influencing other lending rates like the Loan Prime Rates (LPRs). The PBOC uses these open market operations to inject or absorb funds, influencing interbank...

articles.title: S&P and NASDAQ erase declines in trading positive territory
articles.expand: Comments from Israel PM Netanyahu are helping to reverse the trends in oil and stocks and ease some of the wall of worry. Netanyahu is making amends for the bombing of South Pars field yesterday. In retaliation, Iran targeted Qatar’s energy infrastructure—specifically the Ras Laffan Industrial City, which is one of the world’s largest liquefied nat...

articles.title: The US Dollar dives after the FOMC, long-term reversal incoming? – Dollar Index (DXY) outlook
articles.expand: US Dollar (DXY) update: The Petrodollar rally stalls at the top of its July 2025 range as Jerome Powell pushes against further rate hikes during the FOMC meeting. With the Greenback tumbling and its correlation to sideways WTI Crude weakening, a large correction looms. Explore an in-depth technical analysis of the currency.

articles.title: Forex Today: US Dollar Index drops below 100 after central banks hold rates
articles.expand: The US Dollar Index (DXY) fell below the 100 mark and is now trading near the 99.40 price zone after investors assessed the Fed’s interest rate decision. Chair Jerome Powell claimed on Wednesday that higher energy prices will likely lead to inflation.

articles.title: AUD/USD Price Forecast: Aussie turns bullish above the 50-day SMA, eyes 0.7100
articles.expand: AUD/USD advances some 0.47% on Thursday as the US Dollar (USD) weakens, despite traders delaying Federal Reserve (Fed) rate cuts, while US Crude Oil prices are edging lower by 4.21%, a headwind for the buck. The pair hovers around 0.7050 at the time of writing.

articles.title: Gold crashes below $4,600 as US yields spike, Fed cut bets delayed until 2027
articles.expand: Gold (XAU/USD) retreats more than 4.5% on Thursday as US Treasury yields rise amid investor concerns about high energy prices, while a solid US jobs report prompted traders to price out a rate cut in 2026, with expectations of the first move in 2027.

articles.title: Copper miners are pricing in a rising chance of recession today
articles.expand: What's the diagnosis of Dr. Copper? It's not a good one at the moment as the industrial metal sinks today. The price action in copper and other assets today suggests a shift away from an inflationary price shock and towards the risk of central banks raising rates and causing a global recession, or at least contributing to one. articles.title: Dow Jones Industrial Average drops again as Iran war bogs down equity markets
articles.expand: The Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) extended losses on Thursday, falling below 46,000 as a renewed spike in Crude Oil prices deepened concerns about stagflation in the US economy.

articles.title: NZD/USD rises as weaker US Dollar offsets disappointing New Zealand GDP
articles.expand: NZD/USD trades around 0.5840 on Thursday, up 0.73% on the day, rebounding after the previous day’s decline. The pair largely shrugs off disappointing GDP data from New Zealand, instead benefiting from a weaker US Dollar (USD).

articles.title: Australian Dollar Price Forecast: Some consolidation likely before a move to 0.7200?
articles.expand: For the time being, the near-term prospect for the Australian Dollar (AUD) remains positive, supported by consistently high inflation in Australia and the RBA's hawkish stance.

articles.title: USD/JPY drops even as Fed holds hawkish tone
articles.expand: USD/JPY fell to 158.40 on Thursday as investors assessed both nations' central bank decisions. On the one hand, there was the Federal Reserve's (Fed) decision to hold rates steady, with Chair Jerome Powell claiming that higher energy prices will push up overall inflation.

articles.title: ECB: Higher energy risks bring hikes closer – Nordea
articles.expand: Nordea’s Chief Analyst Jan von Gerich says the European Central Bank (ECB) kept rates unchanged but signalled greater readiness to tighten if higher energy prices feed into broader inflation.

articles.title: BoE: Hawkish hold under energy shock – Rabobank
articles.expand: Rabobank’s Senior Macro Strategist Stefan Koopman notes that the Bank of England (BoE) kept Bank Rate at 3.75% but adopted a distinctly hawkish tone as a renewed energy shock lifts inflation projections.

articles.title: GBP: BoE hawkish turn tempers Pound – TD Securities
articles.expand: TD Securities strategists note that a more hawkish Bank of England (BoE), including removal of its easing bias and discussion of potential hikes, produced only modest Pound (GBP) gains versus the Dollar (USD).

articles.title: Bank of England leaves interest rates unchanged as war and rising energy prices complicate inflation fight
articles.expand: The Bank of England kept rates unchanged at 3.75% as war in the Middle East and rising energy prices cloud the inflation outlook and complicate the path for UK monetary policy.

articles.title: BoE unanimous rate hold surprises as doves abandon cut calls amid energy shock
articles.expand: Bank of England left Bank Rate unchanged at 3.75%, but the unanimous vote came as a clear surprise. Markets had expected a 6–3 or 7–2 split, with several known doves continuing to push for rate cuts. Instead, even the most dovish members backed a hold, signaling a sharp shift in near-term policy thinking as the […] The post BoE unanimous rate...

articles.title: BOC Holds Rates at 2.25% As US-Iran War and CUSMA Cloud Outlook
articles.expand: The Bank of Canada kept its policy rate unchanged at 2.25% for the fifth consecutive meeting, citing a deteriorating growth outlook and rising inflation risks stemming from the conflict in the Middle East.
